output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back
output.meta_description: Your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your money back.
content_outline: Why Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (Legal vs. Illegal Reasons)
State Deadlines: How Long Your Landlord Actually Has to Return Your Deposit
What Your Landlord Can and Cannot Legally Deduct
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Demand Letter That Works
Escalating to Small Claims Court: When and How
key_points: Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14–30 days with an itemized list of deductions
Normal wear and tear—such as carpet wear, minor wall scuffs, and faded paint—cannot be deducted
A formal demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recover your deposit without hiring a lawyer
Landlords who miss state deadlines or fail to provide itemized statements may owe double or triple damages
You can file in small claims court for withheld deposits; no attorney is required in most states
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep what's yours. Use DepositBack to generate a legally sound security deposit demand letter in minutes and get your money back.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters dealing with landlords who are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit

social_hooks.hook: POV: Your landlord just charged you $800 for 'cleaning' but you left the apartment spotless
social_hooks.script: This is illegal. Normal wear and tear is not deductible. Here's the 30-second demand letter script that got my $1,400 back without going to court. Save this before they delete it.

social_hooks.hook: My landlord tried to keep my $2,000 deposit for a 'burnt out lightbulb'... so I did this
social_hooks.script: I didn't hire a lawyer. I didn't go to court. I sent this one letter. Seven days later, full deposit plus interest. Link in bio for the template.

social_hooks.hook: 3 things your landlord legally CANNOT deduct from your security deposit (most renters don't know #2)
social_hooks.script: Scuffed walls from picture frames? Not deductible. Faded carpet after three years? Not deductible. Pre-existing damage? Definitely not deductible. If they try, send this letter immediately.

social_hooks.hook: If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit in 30 days, they're banking on you not knowing this
social_hooks.script: In most states, missing the deadline means they owe you the FULL deposit back—sometimes double. Stop texting them. Start documenting. Send a demand letter today.

social_hooks.hook: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Don't post on Reddit. Do this instead.
social_hooks.script: Posting feels good. Getting your money back feels better. This letter takes five minutes and puts the law on your side. Save this before you need it.
